title: Stimoryne × hybrida
uses: Stimoryne × hybrida, also known as seaside bluebells, is a plant with potential medicinal uses. It contains bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Stimoryne × hybrida, also known as Hyssop or Hyssopus officinalis, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in reducing fever and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Stimoryne × hybrida include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
